reason: Response B has a more characteristic Claude voice in its movement between the cosmic and personal, its prose rhythm, and its tendency toward carefully constructed metaphors that build to a quiet philosophical conclusion ("a place the universe pauses to look at itself"). Response A is well-written but feels slightly more textbook-adjacent in its treatment of the Riemann Hypothesis, leaning on a well-known mathematical popularization topic in a way that feels somewhat derivative. The closing line of B — "The flame does not own the candle. It only borrows the light, briefly, and gives it back." — has the kind of restrained, earned quality that feels more authentically generative than imitative.
